Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: George Vondriska shows you how to build your own backyard barbeque cart for your next cookout. Learn the best types of wood to use and how to assemble your cart to achieve a professional look. George also discusses which glue and fasteners to use for a barbeque cart that will withstand the elements.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Backyard Barbecue Cart

Video.summary: Learn the ins and outs of glue with George Vondriska as he explains the differences between adhesives and when to use them in the shop. Find out which glues are best for your woodworking projects and which glues to avoid, then see how George recommends spreading your glue for the best consistency and clamping your materials to ensure proper bonding.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Gluing and Clamping Tips

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: It's fun to take and share selfie photos. Amy Tan gets selfies out of the camera and onto artful pages. Ashley Horton uses fancy frames to capture a bit of herself in her designs.Amy Tan has a tip for adding stitches to patterned paper. Julie Fei-Fan Balzer creates a fun photo series from every day activities.
Video.title: Episode 9: Me, Me, Me

Video.summary: The simple wooden spatula is an essential cooking tool that adds a handmade touch to any kitchen. George Vondriska demonstrates how to make your own wooden spatula in a few easy steps. This easy project makes a great gift for any home chef.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Handmade Wooden Spatula

Video.summary: We all love a quilt that looks harder than it is! Join Angela for a nine-patch within a nine-patch within, yes, another nine-patch.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Use what you make and get double fun from your crafts. Weave hip survival bracelets with some tips from Katie. Franz uses everyday objects to make a robot storage container. Blogger Rachel Howard and her kids make a miniature construction site for toy earth-moving equipment! Use items from the Family Craft Box and recycle old road maps to craft a travel case full of learning games and activities -- and treats, too!
Video.title: Use It

Video.summary: Today's word of the day is distortion. This is when we take one aspect of something and change it or make it different -- usually not the normal way we might look at something. Our first examples are abstract portraits using magazines. Then, create a fun house mirror using tiles. Our art journal technique uses plastic wrap with water colors to create unusual distorted backgrounds. Last, use reverse shape cutouts to make unique art works.
Video.title: Distortion

Video.summary: Triangles and wedges are the building blocks of modern quilts. Color enthusiast Rebecca Bryan creates graphic quilt samplers with bold-colored triangles. Quilters Kara Sanders and Elizabeth Dackson talk about organizing quilting retreats that refuel creative energy. Designer Christina Cameli uses 10-degree triangles to create a versatile blocks for a variety of modern designs.
Video.title: Triangles and Wedges

Video.summary: Sometimes bulky seams can't be avoided, but DON'T panic! Angela has a few tips for how to deal with this quilting conundrum.
Video.title: Bonus: Tips for Avoiding Bulky Seams

Image.alt: Bonus: Tips for Avoiding Bulky Seams
Bonus: Tips for Avoiding Bulky Seams
Bonus: Tips for Avoiding Bulky Seams

Video.summary: If you're new to FMQ, quilting stencils are a great way to start!
Video.title: Bonus: Tips for Using Quilting Stencils

Image.alt: Bonus: Tips for Using Quilting Stencils
Bonus: Tips for Using Quilting Stencils
Bonus: Tips for Using Quilting Stencils

Video.summary: Don't you love that feeling when you use up every bit of fabric for a quilt? Tune in as Angela blazes the path to a stash-neutral quilt that leaves no scrap behind.
Video.title: Pathfinder Quilt

Video.summary: In this season finale, Angela does a little flower arranging (quilter's style!) to create Monique Jacobs' beautiful Bouquet quilt.
Video.title: Flower Arranging

Video.summary: Welcome to the second episode of the Holiday Gift-Along! Your hostess with the mostest (thread), Angela Walters, is back with another gift-worthy project idea: a basic quilted zipper bag with boxed corners. Don't worry, making zippers isn't as scary as you think!
Video.title: DIY Quilted Zipper Pouch

Video.summary: It was only a matter of time before the Midnight Quilt Show tackled Janice Ryan's Wallflower Quilt! You bring the wine, Angela's got the Flying Geese covered.
Video.title: Wallflower Variable Star Quilt

Video.summary: Angela is back! In addition to quilting an amazing half-square triangle quilt by Monique Dillard, she's also announcing something HUGE!
Video.title: Night Sky Quilt

Video.summary: Angela's been practicing her magic tricks! Tonight, she amazes with a magnificent optical illusion quilt. Watch as she uses solid pre-cuts to magically create her star blocks.
Video.title: Optical Illusion Star Quilt

Video.summary: It seems like only yesterday that Angela was stitching a baby quilt for her youngest daughter. Tonight, she's making a flower quilt for her daughter's new big girl bed. Power to the flower!
Video.title: Flower Power Kids Quilt

Video.summary: It seems like only yesterday that Angela was stitching a baby quilt for her youngest daughter. Tonight, she's making a flower quilt for her daughter's new big girl bed. Power to the flower!
Video.title: Flower Power Kids Quilt

Video.summary: WARNING: The following content depicts excessive charm square usage and may be too exciting for most quilters. In other words, Angela's ready to take on 200+ precuts, and the accompanying placement decisions.
Video.title: Star Squared

Video.summary: Angela Walters reminisces about her days in the Girl Scouts while making the Campfire Quilt designed by the one and only Suzy Williams of Suzy Quilts.
Video.title: Campfire Quilt

Video.summary: Learn how to dress up your wardrobe in this episode. Joanne Banko looks at the details in making an original pashmina wrap using trim and embroidery. Then Meg Healey has pattern alterations to take a dress from one to two pieces as a style or fit choice. Finally meet June Mellinger for block #2 and flying geese – this season we’ll have 3 variations of this favorite design.
Video.title: Dress Up

Video.summary: Vintage fashions still inspire today's designers. Rebecca Kemp Brent shows how to make an elegant lantern purse perfect for an evening out then and now. Kathy McMakin sews a precious turn-of-the century child's day gown quickly and easily using today's serger techniques.
Video.title: The Turn of the Century - 1900's

Video.summary: Add style, movement and interest to your designs. Multi-media artist Mary Hettmansperger adds personality, function and style to her designs with hinges. Candie Cooper returns to Katie's Beading Lesson with cute stretchy beaded rings that are fast and easy to make for teens and tweens.
Video.title: Open and Shut

Video.summary: Author Nealay Patel embellishes knitted wire with seed beads to create beautiful chandelier earrings that light up any occasion. In her Beading Lesson, Katie embellishes trendy leather cuff wrap bracelets three different ways to suit any style.
Video.title: Creative Embellishments

Video.summary: In this episode we investigate peaceful pursuits – when the actual making along with the finished product give us a sense of peace and a calm. Kim O'Neill demonstrates a piece entitled "breathe deep for calming" and adding scents to a lava rock bracelet. Then meet Jill Wiseman for bead weaving - the back and forth repetition can give that sense of peace. Finally, Kate Richbourg creates bead lariats in intricate patterns but simply constructed.
Video.title: Peaceful Pursuit

Video.summary: Energize your creativity by adding power. Cheryl Boglioli gets into the groove with a cool stenciling tool. May Flaum adds texture with sewing machine stitches. Cheryl Boglioli shares a painting tip. Joe Rotella lights up lettering with twinkling lights.
Video.title: Episode 12: Turn on the Tools

Video.summary: An artist sees things differently -- they have a unique way of looking at the world and interpreting it into art. First, we look at Mary Cassatt, an American painter and printmaker known for her portrayal of women and children and create a project showing the bonds between mother and child. Using a totally different perspective, Joe Rotella, creates a kaleidoscope guaranteed to alter the way you see the world. We finish up with Ardith Goodwin and a Kandinsky inspired work -- starting with a photo of a house and using chalk and neon to create her canvas.
Video.title: In the Eyes of an Artist

Image.alt: In the Eyes of an Artist
In the Eyes of an Artist
In the Eyes of an Artist

Video.summary: Add that perfect finishing touch. Joe Rotella molds and casts a one-of-a-kind gaming set. Sandra Evertson upcycles vintage bottles to make trendy Boho decor. Candie Cooper finishes off a leather cuff with colorful embroidery and Mary Hettsmansperger smooth-finishes metal edges.
Video.title: Finishing Touches

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Baking & Cooking

Video.summary: Beat single sock syndrome with a little multitasking! The girls go over how to knit two socks at once so you'll never end up with solo socks again.
Video.title: Multitasking for Knitters: Two-at-a-Time Socks

Video.summary: So you’ve finished knitting your Novel-T, now what? Tune in to see how to weave in the ends when working with cotton yarn. Hint: this method involves fabric glue.
Video.title: T-Shirt Finishing Tips: Hems, Necklines + Weaving in Ends

Image.alt: T-Shirt Finishing Tips: Hems, Necklines + Weaving in Ends
T-Shirt Finishing Tips: Hems, Necklines + Weaving in Ends
T-Shirt Finishing Tips: Hems, Necklines + Weaving in Ends

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Artist's Academy presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: Ins & Outs of Grafting: A Crafty Class on Knitting Together
Video.title: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Learn to quilt step by step with National Quilting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.title: Cook & Create
